impact:
EXPOSED (not caused) by removing kDryGain in ISS-008. kDryGain was masking this; with the mask gone the underlying level error is visible and should be fixed properly.

THE PROBLEM. kOutputMakeup[] = {0.444, 0.513, 0.123} was fitted to make the plugin's ABSOLUTE output level match the captures (V1L's comment: "fitted from ab_report.py --filter V1L NULL clean gain +12.4 dB"; V2's is "still the placeholder"). But the captures are NAM-model output, whose absolute in/out level is NORMALIZED AWAY (memory: noamp-capture-pipeline; ISS-008's own text: "kOutputMakeup CANNOT be fitted from gain-matched data at all — it is a global output scalar, exactly what the analysis gain-match removes"). So these values are fitted to an arbitrary per-batch NAM normalization constant, not to a property of the pedal.

WHY IT IS WRONG, PHYSICALLY. The processor applies outputGain = kOutputMakeup[rev]/kInputRef globally (PluginProcessor::outputGainFor), and the DSP works in real volts. So the only physically faithful value is kOutputMakeup = 1.0 (volts in -> volts out at the circuit's own gain). Every deviation is a cosmetic fudge. V2's 0.123 is -18.2 dB, which means the V2 model's output is being attenuated 18 dB below what the circuit says.

THE OBSERVABLE CONSEQUENCE. The dry path has a KNOWN gain from the circuit alone (unity input buffer -> BLEND/LEVEL dividers -> U3B +10.1 dB -> MID -1 -> tone -1 = ~+4 dB, confirmed: V2IntegrationTest now measures +4.18 dB inside the DSP). After the global scalar, a user at BLEND=0 (full dry) on V2 hears +4.18 - 18.2 = ~-14 dB. A bass DI's clean path must pass ~unity — that is the entire point of the dry path. V1E (-7.1 dB) and V1L (-5.8 dB) are less wrong but wrong in the same way, and the three revisions are inconsistent with each other.

WHY IT IS NOT URGENT / WHY IT IS SAFE TO FIX SEPARATELY. kOutputMakeup is a single global scalar, so it CANNOT affect any shape metric — every FR/THD/null comparison in the harness gain-matches first. Changing it is provably shape-neutral, so it cannot regress the kDriveEndR/saturation/zener fits, all of which were fit on gain-matched data. It only affects (a) what a user hears and (b) the ab_report LEVEL column.

THE ANCHOR TO USE. Do NOT re-fit from capture level (impossible — see above). The dry path IS the anchor: it is the one path whose gain is fully determined by the circuit with no fitted constants, so set kOutputMakeup so the modelled dry path comes out at its circuit-derived gain. That points at kOutputMakeup = 1.0 for all three revisions.

WHAT THAT WILL EXPOSE (do this with eyes open). The current makeup RATIO V1E/V2 = 0.444/0.123 = +11.1 dB encodes a real, unexplained claim: that the V2 model runs ~11 dB hotter than the V1E model relative to the pedal. Per the memory, V1E and V2 were IDENTICALLY STAGED, so their RELATIVE level across captures is real evidence — meaning there may be a genuine ~11 dB gain error in one of the two models (suspect: V2's +10.1 dB U3B LEVEL stage, netlists.md V6). Setting both to 1.0 will surface that in the LEVEL column instead of hiding it in a scalar. That is the point: it converts a hidden fudge into a visible, diagnosable gap.

Suggested sequence: set kOutputMakeup to 1.0 for all three -> confirm every shape metric in ab_report.py is unchanged (it must be) -> read the LEVEL column -> investigate the V1E-vs-V2 relative gap as its own issue.
title: kOutputMakeup is mis-anchored: fit to NAM-normalized (meaningless) absolute level; V2's 0.123 leaves the dry path ~18 dB quiet
